skill.name: relight
skill.description: Relight a still image — change the lighting setup, color temperature, direction, or mood — on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes to Qwen Edit 2509's dedicated `relight` LoRA endpoint for purpose-built relighting, with fallback to identity-preserving edit endpoints (Nano Banana 2 Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, FLUX Kontext Pro) when prose lighting language is enough. Use for product relighting (studio softbox → window light), portrait mood shift (overcast → golden hour), or color-grade change. Triggers on "relight", "relighting", "change the lighting", "make it golden hour", "studio lighting", "rim light", "blue hour", "soft window light", "change light direction", "color temperature", or any explicit ask to alter how a still is lit.
skill.summary: Relight still images with precise control over direction, color temperature, and mood via RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate image relighting tool that uses the scoped `Bash(runcomfy *)` permission to invoke the RunComfy CLI. All static analysis findings are false positives: the 63 'backtick execution' detections are Markdown inline code formatting, the 20 'hardcoded URLs' are documentation links, and the 'hidden file' reference points to a properly permissioned config file (mode 0600). The skill includes comprehensive security documentation covering token storage, input boundaries, and outbound endpoint allowlisting. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: External CLI invocation within scoped permission
low_findings.description: The skill uses `Bash(runcomfy *)` to invoke the RunComfy CLI, which makes network requests to RunComfy's Model API. This is the intended functionality and is properly scoped, but represents a network and command execution surface that users should be aware of.

content.value_statement: Professional photographers and e-commerce teams need to change image lighting without expensive reshoots. This skill provides direct access to purpose-built AI relighting models that preserve subject identity while transforming light direction, color temperature, and mood.
actual_capabilities: Relight still images using Qwen Edit 2509's dedicated relight LoRA for precise lighting control
Change lighting direction (camera-left, top-down, rim light from behind)
Adjust color temperature from warm 3200K to cool 6500K
Apply time-of-day presets like golden hour, blue hour, and overcast
Fall back to identity-preserving edit endpoints (Nano Banana 2, GPT Image 2, FLUX Kontext Pro) for composite edits
Batch process multiple images with consistent lighting using image_urls arrays
limitations: Only works with still images — video relighting requires ComfyUI workflows, not this CLI skill
Requires a RunComfy account and API token (obtained via `runcomfy login`)
Best results require detailed lighting prompts specifying direction, temperature, and preservation requirements
Cannot generate images from scratch — only relight existing images

use_cases.title: E-commerce product relighting
use_cases.description: Transform product photos from studio white-box lighting to lifestyle window light or golden hour without reshooting. Maintain catalog consistency across an entire SKU gallery.

use_cases.title: Portrait mood enhancement
use_cases.description: Shift portrait lighting from flat overcast to dramatic golden hour rim lighting while preserving subject identity, pose, and facial features.

use_cases.title: Brand lighting standardization
use_cases.description: Apply a consistent brand lighting signature across hundreds of images, or train a custom relight LoRA to capture a specific studio setup.

prompt_templates.title: Basic product relight
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight the product image at ./product.jpg to soft window light from camera-left with warm 4000K temperature. Preserve the product orientation, color, and framing. Save the result to ./out/.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lighting change for a single product photo

prompt_templates.title: Golden hour portrait
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight ./portrait.jpg as golden hour: warm 3200K key light from camera-left at 45°, soft cool fill from right, rim light from behind. Preserve subject identity, pose, and skin tone.
prompt_templates.scenario: Portrait mood shift to golden hour

prompt_templates.title: Batch catalog relighting
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight all images in ./catalog/ to match studio softbox lighting from above with neutral 5500K temperature. Use Qwen Edit Relight LoRA for consistency. Output to ./relit/.
prompt_templates.scenario: Standardize lighting across a product catalog

prompt_templates.title: Reference-based lighting match
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the lighting from ./reference.jpg (dramatic side light, high contrast) to ./subject.jpg using GPT Image 2 Edit. Preserve subject identity completely.
prompt_templates.scenario: Match lighting from a reference photo

output_examples.input: Relight product.jpg to warm window light from left, preserve product colors
output: Successfully relit product.jpg using Qwen Edit 2509 Relight LoRA
Lighting applied: warm window light, camera-left, 4000K
Subject preservation: verified (pose, color, framing maintained)

output_examples.input: Transform portrait.jpg to golden hour with rim light
output: Lighting: golden hour, warm 3200K key, cool fill, rim from behind

best_practices: Always specify preservation requirements in your prompt (pose, identity, color, framing) to prevent model drift
Use the Qwen Edit Relight LoRA for dedicated lighting work; fall back to general edit endpoints only for composite changes
Quantify lighting parameters explicitly — direction (45°), temperature (3200K), and intensity (soft/hard) for reproducible results
Process test images first before batching to verify the lighting matches your intent
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for video relighting — it only supports still images and will fail
Avoid vague prompts like 'make it look better' — specify concrete lighting parameters for reliable results
Do not attempt to generate images from scratch with this skill — use the ai-image-generation skill instead

faq.question: What models does this skill use for relighting?
faq.answer: The default is Qwen Edit 2509's dedicated relight LoRA, which is purpose-built for lighting changes. Fallback options include Nano Banana 2 Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, and FLUX Kontext Pro for composite edits.

faq.question: Can I relight videos with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports still images. For video relighting, you would need to use ComfyUI workflows with IC-Light variants, which are not accessible via the RunComfy CLI.

faq.question: How do I get a RunComfy API token?
faq.answer: Run `runcomfy login` after installing the CLI. The token is stored in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600 permissions. For CI environments, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: Will the subject's identity be preserved?
faq.answer: Yes, the relight models are designed to preserve subject identity, pose, and framing. Include explicit preservation instructions in your prompt for best results.

faq.question: What lighting parameters can I control?
faq.answer: You can control light source type (window, studio, spotlight), color temperature (3200K warm to 6500K cool), direction (camera-left, top-down, rim), and intensity (soft, hard, high-contrast).

faq.question: Is there a file size limit for output images?
faq.answer: Yes, the CLI aborts any single download larger than 2 GiB. This is a safety limit to prevent resource exhaustion.
